Terra Energy·Inspection Guide · AHJ Reference

Texas Solar Inspection Guide
AHJ Reference

Complete solar inspection scheduling guide · AHJ reference for all 1,633 Texas jurisdictions — permit requirements, inspection types, TPI process, and onsite inspection workflows.

✦ 1,633 AHJ Entries
12 Regions
Updated June 2026
SB 1202 Statewide
1
Introduction

This guide covers all 1,633 Texas AHJ entries — cities, counties, and unincorporated areas — with permit requirements, inspection types, and scheduling methods for solar PV installations.

Data is sourced from direct AHJ research and user-provided verification. Always confirm with the AHJ portal before submitting — requirements can change without notice.

2
How to Use This Guide

Use the Texas AHJ Directory in Part 2 to search any city or county. Filter by region, difficulty, or permit count. Click any AHJ card to expand full details — including inspection requirements, onsite documents, scheduling method, and rep requirements.

Use the region filter to narrow results to your service area. The search bar matches by city name, county, region, or utility provider.

3
SB 1202 & TPI

Texas Senate Bill 1202 (effective September 1, 2025) authorizes licensed third-party inspection agencies like Texas Power Inspections (TPI) to conduct plan reviews in place of the AHJ statewide.

This means virtual video-call inspections are available for virtually every jurisdiction in this guide. Rep required: a licensed contractor or representative must be present on-site during all inspections.

Part 2
Texas AHJ Directory
📐
Layout Policy · Applies to Every AHJ Below
Texas solar inspectors are strict on layout matching the approved plans. Small adjustments within the same roof area — panels shifted slightly around an obstruction — are typically accepted as long as the total panel count and per-side distribution remain the same. Large layout changes (different roof faces, changed panel counts per side, different totals) will fail the inspection. When in doubt, file a Plan Revision before scheduling.
📋
AHJ Reference Data
Texas AHJ Reference — 1,633 Municipalities
Click to expand · Search, filter, and view per-AHJ permit & inspection details
▼
Filter: 1,633 results
1,633 municipalities · Texas · Always verify current requirements with the AHJ portal before submitting
5
Inspection Types
Final

Single final inspection at end of project — covers building + electrical in one visit. Most common in standard AHJs.

Prelim / Rough

Preliminary or rough-in inspection before panels are fully covered — typically required in 2-inspection AHJs.

Electrical Final

Electrical-specific final inspection, sometimes separate from the building/structural final in 2-permit jurisdictions.

Verify with AHJ

Variable or unconfirmed requirements — always call or check the AHJ portal directly before scheduling.

6
Onsite Documents

Standard documents required for virtually all Texas solar inspections:

✓  Approved/stamped plan set
✓  Permit card(s) posted
✓  NEC 690 labels per 2023 NEC
✓  Equipment spec sheets
✓  Ladder + safe roof access
✓  Main panel accessible
+ Battery systems: NEC 706 labels, NFPA 855 docs, UL 9540 cert
Part 3
Scheduling
7
TPI Inspection Scheduling Process

Complete step-by-step process for scheduling and coordinating on-site inspections through Texas Power Inspections — from CPQ verification through inspection day video call. Click any step to expand the full checklist and reference screenshots.

1
Check CPQ — Section 5: Private Party Plan Review
Confirm the project is approved and submitted to TPI before proceeding
CPQ
▼
📍
Navigate in CPQ to Section 5 — 03.3 Private Party Plan Review
✓
Verify "Submit for TPI" task is marked COMPLETED — this confirms the project has been submitted to Texas Power Inspections
✅
The Submit for TPI task must be Completed before proceeding. Do not schedule with TPI until this is confirmed.
🖥 CPQ — Section 5: 03.3 Private Party Plan Review
Red arrows indicate Section 5 and the "Submit for TPI" task — both must show green completed checkmarks before proceeding.
2
Verify Permit Package — Section 6: Submittals
Confirm approved plans and permit are on file and ready to share with TPI
CPQ
▼
📍
Navigate to Section 6 — 04 Submittals in CPQ
✓
Expand "Submit Permit Package to the AHJ" to find the Permit Package subtask
📄
Download and confirm you have both: Approved Plans and Approved Permit
🔍
Open the Permitting panel — confirm Permit Status = Issued & Received and note the Master Permit Number
🔍
Verify PPI Vendor and PPR Vendor both show Texas Power Inspections
🖥 CPQ — Section 6: 04 Submittals → Permit Package
Arrow points to Permit Package subtask. Status must show Completed with attached documents.
🖥 CPQ — Permitting Panel: Vendor & Permit Number
PPI Vendor and PPR Vendor must both show Texas Power Inspections.
3
Check Last Visit Date & Identify Crew
Find Electrical Installation work order, visit date, crew details, and chatter notes
CPQ
▼
Where to look in CPQ
🔍
In CPQ, navigate to Section 11 — 09 Under Construction and find the Electrical Installation work order. Status shows CLOCKED OUT once the crew is working on the visit.
📅
Open the work order and scroll to Visits — note the scheduled date and time (e.g. 05/11/2026 1:00PM). This is the date you align the TPI inspection to.
👥
Note the Assignee — that's your crew member (e.g. NSITX 2 – Davittin Nathong)
💬
Check the chatter / comments for any scheduler notes on the inspection alignment
📞
Collect crew's phone number — required for the TPI portal as on-site contact
📧
Confirm crew's email — added as guest for the TPI calendar invite
🗺
Note the project city / AHJ — determines if a special engineer letter is required
⚠️
TPI inspection date must align with the crew's last visit date. Crew will be physically on-site during the video call with the TPI inspector.
🖥 CPQ Section 11 — Electrical Installation task
The Electrical Installation task showing CLOCKED OUT status — this means the crew is actively working on the visit.
🖥 Electrical Installation work order — Visits & Crew
Red boxes show Assignee (NSITX 2 Davittin Nathong), Visits section (last visit 05/11/2026), and chatter confirmation note.
4
Schedule Inspection on TPI Portal
Book the inspection, add crew contact details, send calendar invite
TPI
▼
🌐
Log in to the TPI portal (Inspection Request link on the TPI website). Contact: Info@TexasPowerInspections.com | 214-326-7077
📅
Set the inspection date to match the crew's last visit date from Step 3
👤
Enter the crew member's full name and phone number as the on-site contact
📧
Add the crew member's email in the Recipients / Guest field so they receive the calendar invite
📹
Confirm inspection format is video call — TPI inspector connects remotely with crew on site
✅
Submit the request and wait for the email confirmation from TPI
📬
After submitting, you will receive an email confirmation from TPI. Save it as proof of scheduled inspection date and time.
Final step — mark Schedule Texas PPI as completed in CPQ
✅
Return to CPQ Section 11 — mark "Schedule Texas PPI" as COMPLETED
🖥 CPQ Section 11 — Schedule Texas PPI (mark Completed)
Once TPI inspection is booked, mark Schedule Texas PPI as Completed. The next task is Meet Inspector on Site/Video Call.
5
Set Up & Notify Crew — Meet Inspector on Site/Video Call
Assign crew, add due date, schedule visit, add instructions, confirm in chatter
CPQ
▼
Open the work order in CPQ Section 11
🔍
Go to Section 11 — 09 Under Construction and open "Meet Inspector on Site/Video Call"
What to fill in
👥
Change the Assignee to the specific crew member doing the electrical installation (e.g. NSITX 2 — Davittin Nathong). If Ryan Hildreth provides a different crew, use that one.
📅
Add the Due Date — set to match the last visit date from Step 3
📍
In the Visits section, click + Visit and add the inspection date and time
📝
Add Instructions for the crew (TPI contact info, what to prepare, access requirements)
💬
Leave a chatter comment tagging the crew (@Name) to confirm the inspection date and calendar invite
🚫
Do not skip this step. Setting up this work order is the official internal record that the crew is confirmed and prepared.
🖥 CPQ Section 11 — Meet Inspector on Site/Video Call
Meet Inspector on Site/Video Call showing ASSIGNED status with crew N2.
🖥 Work order detail — Assignee, Visits & Instructions
Change Assignee to the specific crew member. Click + Visit to add the TPI inspection date and time.
6
Send Approved Documents to TPI via Email
Approved plans, permit, and required engineer letters must reach TPI before inspection day
Email
▼
📤
Email TPI with all required documents — TPI inspector and crew must work from the same set of approved documents
📄
Attach: Approved Plans (from Permit Package in Section 6)
📄
Attach: Approved Permit (from Permit Package in Section 6)
📜
If city requires it (e.g. Houston): attach the sealed/stamped Engineer Letter from Matthew
🏛
TPI will coordinate directly with the municipality (AHJ/muni) to submit and close the inspection
⚠️
City-specific letters: Houston requires the Houston Letter. Missing required documentation will cause the inspection to fail at the AHJ level.
7
Inspection Day — Video Call with TPI Inspector & Crew
TPI inspector connects remotely; crew on site; muni approves and closes
TPI
▼
📹
Inspection is via video call — TPI inspector joins remotely while crew is physically at the property
🏠
Crew must be present at the property and ready at the scheduled time
🏛
TPI coordinates with the municipality (AHJ/muni) to submit, approve, and close the inspection
✓
Once approved, TPI provides inspection closure documentation
📁
Update CPQ project status and file all closure documents
🛑
TPI does NOT check the roof array on inspection day. The TPI inspector reviews electrical equipment via video call only — they do not climb up to verify the roof-mounted PV array.
🎯
TPI acts as the bridge between Terra Energy and the municipality. Your crew provides on-site access; TPI handles all official submission and approval with the AHJ.
8
Tips & Best Practices
✓  Always verify with the AHJ portal before scheduling — requirements change without notice.
✓  For VERIFY or DIFFICULT AHJs, call the jurisdiction directly before submitting any permit application.
✓  SB 1202 virtual inspections are available statewide — but some municipalities may still require an in-person component.
✓  Houston City requires a Houston Letter from Matthew R. Craig P.E. — request via CPQ work order tagged to Jesus.
9
Cancellation & Rescheduling

How to cancel or reschedule a TPI inspection due to unforeseen circumstances, crew changes, or date conflicts.

❌
Cancelling an Inspection
Option 1 — Via calendar link: Open the TPI confirmation email or calendar invite. Click the Cancel link (Calendly), enter the reason, and submit.

Option 2 — Call TPI directly: Contact Texas Power Inspections by phone.
⚠️
Cancel as early as possible. If already submitted to the city, TPI needs to notify the municipality.
🔄
Rescheduling an Inspection
Option 1 — Via calendar link: Click the Reschedule link in the TPI invite (Calendly). Select a new date and time aligned with crew's updated visit.

Option 2 — Call TPI directly: Contact TPI by phone to request a new inspection date.
📅
Confirm the new date aligns with the crew's updated visit schedule in CPQ before selecting a time slot.
Step 1 — Find the cancel or reschedule link in your calendar invite
📅 Google Calendar — TPI inspection event with Cancel & Reschedule links
The TPI calendar invite contains inspection details and two Calendly links at the bottom: Cancel and Reschedule.
Step 2a — Cancelling via Calendly
📅 Calendly Cancel Event
Click Cancel in the calendar invite. Enter the reason and submit. TPI will be notified immediately.
Step 2b — Rescheduling via Calendly
📅 Calendly Reschedule Event
Select a new date and time matching the crew's updated visit. Available slots shown in blue.
📝
After any cancellation or reschedule: Update CPQ — leave a chatter note explaining the reason, update the visit date on the "Meet Inspector on Site/Video Call" work order, and notify the crew.
10
TPI Official Guide — How to Schedule

Official TPI process for requesting and scheduling inspections through Texas Power Inspections.

📍
Texas Power Inspections
Official inspection vendor for TPI projects
Email
Info@TexasPowerInspections.com
Phone
214-326-7077
Inspections
Inspections@texaspowerinspections.com
1
Submit Inspection Request via Web Link
Required for all inspections
▼

Use the Inspection Request link on the TPI website. Include complete onsite contact info: date/time, onsite contact name, project address, permit number, phone, email.

ℹ️
If you have already purchased the Combined Plan Review + Inspection package, you may skip Step 2.
📄 TPI Guide Page 1 — Date/Time Picker
2
Provide Project Documentation
For Inspection Only projects (not combined package)
▼

Send a separate email to Inspections@texaspowerinspections.com with: Customer Name, Project Number/PO, AHJ, Utility Provider, Stamped Plan Set, Structural Letter (if applicable).

⚠️
Do not submit multiple projects in same email thread. Incomplete submissions are returned for correction.
📄 TPI Guide Page 2 — Contact Form & Documentation
3
Inspection Day — Time Expectations & Onsite Requirements
Crew must be ready 5–10 minutes before scheduled time
▼

TPI initiates the inspection via virtual software 5–10 minutes prior to scheduled time. Crew must ensure:

⚡
All electrical and solar equipment fully accessible and open (disconnects, combiner boxes, inverters, batteries, panels)
🔒
All equipment properly bonded and secured
🏷
All required code-compliant labeling installed prior to inspection
🏠
Homeowner or property rep must be present to provide access (including attic if applicable)
🚫
If conditions are not met at inspection time, the inspection may be rescheduled and subject to additional fees.
📄 TPI Guide Page 3 — Onsite Requirements
11
City Requirements — Engineer Letters

Some Texas cities require an additional sealed engineer letter before TPI can submit the inspection.

🏙 Houston (City of Houston)
Houston Letter Required
Houston Letter workflow:

1. In CPQ, create a Work Order
2. Select "Create Justification Letter" — rename to "Create Houston Letter"
3. Tag Jesus to action the letter request
4. Once the sealed letter from Matthew is received, include it in the TPI document email (Step 6)
📌
Do not send documents to TPI until the Houston Letter is sealed by Matthew.
🏙 Other Cities / AHJs
Check with AHJ or Ryan Hildreth
Requirements vary by jurisdiction. Check with the AHJ directly or contact Ryan Hildreth to confirm whether a special engineer letter or additional documentation is required before scheduling with TPI.
12
Houston Letter — Example

Example of a completed sealed Houston Letter from Matthew R. Craig, P.E. Use as a reference when requesting or reviewing.

📄 Houston Letter — Post Installation Special Inspection
Required for Houston AHJ
Verify on every Houston Letter: Permit number matches CPQ · Project address correct · Inspection date present · Digital PE stamp from Matthew R. Craig (No. 157776) · Addressed to City of Houston Building Code Enforcement
13
TPI Letter of Compliance — Example

Example of TPI's official Letter of Compliance — issued by Nicholas Kapalla, confirming plans comply with I-Codes, NEC, and SB 1202.

📄 TPI Letter of Compliance (Plan Review & Approval)
Issued by TPI
When you receive this letter from TPI: Include it in the document email to the crew and AHJ (Step 6). Verify project address, project type, and contractor name match CPQ before sending.
14
Customer Communication Guidelines

Key scenarios from the Customer Communication SOP for inspection coordination.

✅
Scheduled — No Interior Access
Confirm date, clarify access to roof and external equipment, confirm customer presence is not required.
🏠
Interior Access Required
Contact at least 1 week in advance. Provide week-range availability. Never schedule without confirmation.
⏰
No Response in 24 Hours
Send follow-up. Attempt phone after second message. If inspection was booked, cancel with the city.
❌
Inspection Failed / No Access
Contact same day. Explain reason, request availability, mark as high priority.
📅
Installation Outside Business Hours
If installation completed on Saturday, Sunday, or after hours, inspection cannot be same day. Coordinate with homeowner for a business-day inspection.
📞
Always introduce yourself: "This is [Your Name] with Terra Energy." Begin calls with "How are you today?" and "Is now a good time to speak?" Escalate unresolved questions to the PM, or to Sophia (Project Manager Lead) if PM follow-up is insufficient.
Part 5
Onsite (Non-TPI) Inspection
15
Onsite (Non-TPI) Inspection Process

Not every AHJ is eligible for TPI inspection. When TPI is not applicable, you must schedule a traditional onsite inspection with the AHJ directly.

Is the project eligible for TPI?
✅ TPI Eligible
Solar + Battery
TPI only accepts Solar + Battery (ESS) installations. If the project was reviewed by TPI (PPR), it can be scheduled through the TPI portal for a virtual video-call inspection.

How to verify: Check the project in CPQ — confirm PPR Vendor = Texas Power Inspections in the Permitting panel.
⚠️ Not TPI Eligible
Solar-Only
TPI does NOT review or inspect solar-only installations. If the project is solar-only (no battery), it was not reviewed by TPI — you must schedule an onsite inspection directly with the AHJ.

Action: Check the project. If not reviewed by TPI, schedule the inspection through the AHJ portal, phone, or email.
🔍
Always check first: Before scheduling, verify whether the project was reviewed by TPI. Solar+Battery → TPI. Solar-only → onsite inspection via the AHJ directly.
How to schedule an onsite inspection

The scheduling method depends on the AHJ. Use the Texas AHJ Directory (Part 2) to look up the specific city — the "How to Request" field tells you which method that AHJ accepts.

🌐
Online Portal
Most cities use an online portal. Log in with the AHJ-specific credentials and submit the inspection request.
📞
Phone
Some AHJs only accept inspection requests by phone. Call during business hours and provide permit number, address, and contact info.
📧
Email
A few AHJs accept email requests. Send permit number, project address, requested date/time, and onsite contact details.
AHJ portal login credentials — BIA 2
🔑
BIA 2 — AHJ Login Repository
Find usernames and passwords for every online AHJ portal
Open BIA 2
v2.biaenergy.ai ↗

For any AHJ that requires online submission, the login credentials are stored in BIA 2 AHJ at v2.biaenergy.ai. Locate the city in the BIA 2 directory to find the matching username and password before submitting the inspection request.

Cancellation methods & fees

Cancellation for non-TPI onsite inspections varies by AHJ — and some cities charge fees for late cancellations.

📨 Cancellation Methods
Each AHJ has different cancellation rules. Common methods:
• Email — most common
• Phone call — for AHJs without an online cancel option
• AHJ portal — log in and cancel directly
Check the "How to Request" field in the AHJ Directory — the same method usually applies to cancellation.
💵 Cancellation Fees
Sometimes required
Some AHJs charge a fee if the inspection is cancelled on the day of inspection (same-day cancellation).

To avoid fees: Cancel as early as possible — ideally 24+ hours before the scheduled inspection.
⚠️
Same-day cancellation fees apply at many AHJs. Always cancel as early as possible.
Day-of inspection coordination

Onsite inspections require active coordination on inspection day — unlike TPI's scheduled video call.

☀️
Check status in the morning on inspection day. Contact your representative to confirm everything is on track.
⏰
Help get an ETA from the inspector. Inspectors often don't give exact arrival times — call the muni or ask your rep to follow up.
📞
Call the municipality if no ETA is provided by mid-morning. The dispatch desk can usually give a window.
👥
Coordinate with the crew on site — ensure they remain available until the inspector arrives.
📲
Stay in contact with the crew and the inspector's dispatch throughout the day. Onsite inspections are less predictable than TPI video calls.
Photo documentation — attic & roof array

Many AHJs do not send an inspector into the home or onto the roof. To prove the installation meets code, the crew must capture clear photos that can be forwarded to the city inspector.

🏚 Attic Photos
When inspector won't enter home
Crew must capture clear photos of:
• Rafter penetrations — how rails attach to structure
• Wiring runs through the attic
• Junction boxes and conduit transitions
🏠 Roof Array Photos
When inspector won't go on roof
Crew must capture clear photos of:
• Panel layout matching the approved plan
• Attachments and mounting hardware
• Flashing and waterproofing
📸
Send these photos to your representative so they can be forwarded to the city inspector as proof of compliance. Without these, the AHJ may delay or fail the inspection.
Before-inspection checklist (21 items)

Opcional se recomiendan pero no son obligatorios. Haga clic en cualquier fila para marcarla como revisada.">Verify every item below is photographed and confirmed before scheduling the onsite inspection. Items marked Optional are recommended but not mandatory. Click any row to mark it as reviewed.

01
House number on the property matches the approved plan
02
Installation personnel anchored or wearing a safety harness Optional
03
Ladder properly anchored Optional
04
Copper grounding cable visible and connected to the rails
05
J-Box open — showing internal circuits
06
J-Box closed — with its corresponding label
07
Panels installed matching the approved plan (layout and quantity)
08
Panel label showing brand, model, and specifications
09
Inverters installed matching the approved plan (quantity and capacity)
10
Inverters open — showing internal wiring
11
Inverter labels showing brand, model, and specifications
12
Roof conduit properly installed and labeled
13
Wall conduit properly installed and labeled
14
Attic conduit (if applicable) properly installed and labeled
15
Combiner box open — breakers match the capacity specified in the plan
16
AC disconnect open — fuses match the capacity specified in the plan
17
Interconnection point visible and matching the plan
18
Main breaker (exterior and interior panel) — capacity matches the plan
19
AC disconnect labels showing voltage and amperage ratings
20
Utility meter
21
Full system installed with all required labels per the approved plan
✅
All 21 items must be photographed and verified before scheduling. This ensures a smooth, first-time-pass onsite inspection.
Layout policy — what passes vs. what fails

Texas onsite inspectors verify the installed array matches the approved plans. The rule of thumb:

✅ Will pass
Small adjustments
Small adjustments within the same roof area — panels shifted slightly to avoid an obstruction (vent, pipe, skylight).

Conditions:
• Total panel count stays the same
• Per-side distribution stays the same
• Panels remain on the same roof face
❌ Will fail
Big changes
Significant layout changes from the approved plan will cause the inspection to fail.

Examples that fail:
• Panels moved to a different roof face
• Changed panel count per side
• Total panel count differs from plan
For these cases, file a Plan Revision before inspection.
📐
Bottom line: Texas inspectors are strict on layout. Small in-area adjustments due to obstructions are usually accepted; anything that changes the totals or roof-face distribution will fail. When in doubt, request a plan revision before scheduling.
16
Post-Installation Validation (Pictures Only / Onsite)

When inspections are completed as Pictures Only appointments, a Post-Installation Validation must be completed before scheduling. This step is critical to confirm the project is compliant and ready for inspection.

⚠️
This applies to onsite inspections only — not to TPI. TPI's video-call format means photos are not pre-submitted in the same way.
Purpose
✅
Verify Match
Confirm the installed system matches the latest approved plans before scheduling.
🛡️
Prevent Failures
Avoid inspection failures and delays caused by technical non-compliance.
🔍
Catch Issues Early
Identify and resolve discrepancies before the inspection is booked.
Handling Discrepancies

If a discrepancy is found, the action depends on severity:

📄
Hardware Change Letter — when installed hardware differs from approved plans
📝
Justification Letter — limited cases where minor variations need to be explained
🔄
Plan Revision — when changes are significant enough to require updated approved drawings
📸
Request missing evidence from the field team if photos are incomplete
1
Verify the Solar Inspection Checklist in Coperniq
Confirm all photos uploaded under the Solar Installation WO
▼

Before scheduling, confirm the installation team has uploaded all required photos to the Solar Installation WO under the Solar Inspection Checklist, located in the Under Construction stage.

📍
Navigate to "Under Construction" stage in Coperniq — where the Solar Installation WO is located
✓
Open the Solar Installation WO → Solar Inspection Checklist — verify status shows COMPLETED
📸
All photos must be present and complete in the Solar Inspection Checklist before proceeding
🛑
Do not proceed to Step 2 if any photos are missing — request them from the field team first.
2
Open the Post-Installation Validation WO
Verify all required photos are present
▼

Open the Post-Installation Validation WO in the current phase in Coperniq and verify that all required photos are present.

📍
Locate the "Inspections" stage — where the Post-Installation Validation WO is located
✓
Create or open the "Post-Installation Validation" WO in the current phase
📸
Review and verify all required photos (see checklist below)
✅
Once all photos are reviewed and confirmed, the WO status will move to COMPLETED
Required Photos Checklist (21 items)

Verify all of the following photos are present. Items marked Optional are recommended but not mandatory. Click any item to mark it as reviewed.

01

02
Installation personnel anchored or wearing a safety harness Optional
03
Ladder properly anchored Optional
04
Copper grounding cable visible and connected to the rails
05
J-Box open — showing internal circuits
06
J-Box closed — with its corresponding label
07
Panels installed matching the approved plan (layout and quantity)
08
Panel label showing brand, model, and specifications
09
Inverters installed matching the approved plan (quantity and capacity)
10
Inverters open — showing internal wiring
11
Inverter labels showing brand, model, and specifications
12
Roof conduit properly installed and labeled
13
Wall conduit properly installed and labeled
14
Attic conduit (if applicable) properly installed and labeled
15
Combiner box open — breakers match the capacity specified in the plan
16
AC disconnect open — fuses match the capacity specified in the plan
17
Interconnection point visible and matching the plan
18
Main breaker (exterior and interior panel) — capacity matches the plan
19
AC disconnect labels showing voltage and amperage ratings
20

3
Mark WO as Complete & Schedule
Only schedule the inspection after validation is complete
▼
✅
Once all photos are verified and correct, mark the Post-Installation Validation WO as Complete in Coperniq
📅
Only after the WO is marked complete, proceed to schedule the inspection (online portal, phone, or email per the AHJ)
🎯
Only schedule the inspection after the Post-Installation Validation WO is marked complete. This ensures a smooth, first-time-pass inspection.
Part 6
Reference
17
Glossary of Terms

Key terms and abbreviations used throughout this guide.

AHJ
Authority Having Jurisdiction
The local government body — typically the city or county building department — that reviews permit applications and approves inspections.
TPI
Texas Power Inspections
Terra Energy's third-party inspection vendor. Schedules virtual inspections and coordinates with the AHJ.
CPQ
Configure, Price, Quote — Project Management
Terra's internal project management system where all tasks, work orders, visits, and permits are tracked.
PPI
Private Party Inspector
The third-party inspection vendor assigned. In CPQ, the PPI Vendor field should show Texas Power Inspections.
PPR
Private Party Review
Plan review by a licensed third-party agency (TPI) instead of the AHJ, authorized under Texas SB 1202.
Muni
Municipality
The local city or jurisdiction. TPI works with the muni to submit, track, and close inspections.
NEC
National Electrical Code
US standard for safe electrical installation. All solar PV systems must comply with NEC 690 and 706.
ASCE 7-22
Structural Engineering Standard
Specifies minimum design loads — used for wind speed requirements (132 mph for Houston). Referenced in sealed letters.
SB 1202
Texas Senate Bill 1202
Effective Sep 1, 2025 — authorizes third-party agencies like TPI to conduct plan reviews statewide.
PV
Photovoltaic
Technology that converts sunlight into electricity. Solar panels are PV modules.
ESS
Energy Storage System
Battery storage paired with solar. Adds NEC 706, NFPA 855, and UL 9540 requirements.
PE
Professional Engineer
A licensed engineer whose stamp is legally required for structural compliance. Matthew R. Craig P.E. handles Houston Letters.
NABCEP
North American Board of Certified Energy Practitioners
Industry certification for solar professionals. TPI's Nicholas Kapalla is NABCEP Board Certified.
ICC
International Code Council
Publishes the I-Codes. TPI's representative holds ICC E1 (Residential Electrical Inspector) certification.
NSITX / N2
National Solar Installers of Texas — Crew 2
Terra's Houston-area installation crew. In CPQ, shown as "NSITX 2 – Davittin Nathong".
Clocked Out
CPQ Work Order Status
In CPQ, "Clocked Out" on the Electrical Installation work order means the crew is actively working on the visit.
